breaking_news.title: State-Linked APTs Shift to Active OT Disruption, Mapping Industrial Control Systems
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Multiple state-affiliated threat groups—including Voltzite (linked to Volt Typhoon) and Kamacite—have escalated from persistent access to active operational technology (OT) disruption planning. Voltzite manipulated engineering workstations in U.S. energy and pipeline networks to map conditions that could trigger process shutdowns, advancing to Stage 2 of Dragos’ ICS Cyber Kill Chain. Kamacite shifted from corporate supply-chain targeting to directly scanning U.S. industrial control devices for four months, mapping specific control loops to enable physical disruption.

Paragraph 2: This shift represents a dangerous evolution in nation-state cyber operations, with fewer than 10% of OT networks possessing detection capabilities for such activity. The implications are severe: attackers now possess the precision and intent to cause physical damage, disrupt critical infrastructure, and trigger cascading safety incidents. Organizations in energy, water, and manufacturing face unprecedented risk as these groups mature from espionage to sabotage.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit—23 Exploits Across 5 Chains—Now Used in Global Espionage and Crypto Theft
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Google’s Threat Intelligence Group identified the Coruna iOS exploit kit—a sophisticated, spyware-grade toolset comprising five full exploit chains and 23 individual exploits targeting iOS 13 through 17.2.1. Coruna leverages unpatched vulnerabilities in WebContent, PAC, and sandbox escape components, including CVE-2021-30952, CVE-2022-48503, and CVE-2023-32409. Initially used by Russian state actors, it has since proliferated to financially motivated threat actors conducting crypto theft and surveillance campaigns.

Paragraph 2: The kit’s widespread deployment—now observed in campaigns targeting Ukrainian users and crypto wallets—demonstrates how advanced nation-state capabilities rapidly leak into broader criminal use. With no patch available for iOS 17.2.1 and earlier, millions of devices remain vulnerable. Apple’s failure to support older OS versions leaves users exposed, and the exploit’s modular architecture enables easy adaptation for new targets. This represents one of the most comprehensive iOS attack toolkits ever disclosed.

breaking_news.title: Global Takedown of Tycoon2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Platform Disrupts MFA Bypass at Scale
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Microsoft, Europol, and international law enforcement partners dismantled Tycoon2FA, one of the world’s largest phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platforms. The operation seized 330 domains via a U.S. court order and disrupted infrastructure across six European countries. Tycoon2FA enabled adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) attacks that bypassed multi-factor authentication (MFA), accounting for ~62% of all phishing campaigns by mid-2025 and sending over 500,000 fraudulent emails monthly.

Paragraph 2: The takedown significantly disrupts credential harvesting campaigns targeting enterprises, financial institutions, and government agencies. However, the platform’s modular architecture and widespread adoption mean copycat services are likely to emerge quickly. This highlights the persistent threat of PhaaS in modern attacks: even with successful takedowns, the underlying business model remains resilient. Organizations must accelerate adoption of phishing-resistant authentication (e.g., passkeys, FIDO2) to mitigate ongoing AitM risks.

breaking_news.title: LeakBase Cybercrime Forum Seized by FBI and Europol—142,000+ Members Compromised
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: A joint FBI–Europol operation dismantled LeakBase, one of the world’s largest underground forums for trading stolen credentials, exploits, and cybercrime tools. As of December 2025, the forum hosted over 142,000 members and 215,000 messages, facilitating the exchange of data from breaches across critical sectors—including healthcare, finance, and government. Users could purchase access to compromised corporate networks, RMM tools, and zero-day exploits.

Paragraph 2: The shutdown disrupts a central hub of the cyber underground and may temporarily reduce the availability of stolen data on the open market. However, forums like LeakBase are highly resilient; operators often migrate to encrypted Telegram channels or darknet markets. The operation underscores the systemic risk posed by credential markets: leaked data fuels credential stuffing, supply chain attacks, and initial access brokering for ransomware. Organizations must enforce strict password hygiene, MFA, and continuous monitoring for compromised credentials.
threat_actors: Unknown forum operators; broad spectrum of cybercriminals

breaking_news.title: Cisco Fixes Maximum-Severity Secure FMC Bugs Allowing Unauthenticated Root Access
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Cisco patched two maximum-severity vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-20079 and CVE-2026-20131) in its Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC), a centralized platform for managing Cisco firewalls. CVE-2026-20079 is a CVSS 10.0 authentication bypass flaw allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary scripts and gain root access. CVE-2026-20131 is a command injection vulnerability in the FMC’s web interface. Exploitation would grant full control over managed firewalls, enabling traffic manipulation, data interception, or denial of service.

Paragraph 2: Given FMC’s role as the central command node for enterprise firewall fleets, compromise enables massive lateral movement and persistent control across network perimeters. CISA is expected to add CVE-2026-20079 to its KEV catalog shortly, triggering urgent patching mandates for federal agencies and critical infrastructure. Organizations using Cisco Secure Firewall should prioritize patching and isolate FMC from untrusted networks until updated.
threat_actors: Unknown (likely APTs or high-skill threat actors)

breaking_news.title: FreeScout Mail Server Compromised via Mail2Shell Zero-Click RCE Vulnerability
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: A maximum-severity vulnerability in the FreeScout helpdesk platform enables zero-click remote code execution (RCE) without user interaction or authentication. The flaw—tracked as CVE-2026-XXXX (not yet assigned)—allows attackers to achieve full server compromise by sending a specially crafted email. Proof-of-concept exploits have been observed in the wild, with attackers deploying webshells and credential harvesters.

Paragraph 2: FreeScout is widely used by enterprises and government agencies for customer support and internal ticketing. A zero-click RCE in such a platform is exceptionally dangerous: attackers can achieve persistent access, pivot to adjacent systems, and exfiltrate sensitive communications. Organizations running FreeScout must patch immediately or disable email ingestion until a fix is applied. This vulnerability exemplifies the growing risk of unauthenticated attack surfaces in enterprise web applications.

breaking_news.title: OAuth Redirect Abuse Lets Attackers Bypass Email Defenses and Deliver Malware
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Attackers are exploiting a built-in OAuth protocol feature—legitimate redirect behavior during error conditions—to bypass email and browser phishing protections. By crafting URLs that appear to originate from Microsoft Entra ID or Google Workspace, attackers redirect victims to malicious infrastructure without ever stealing tokens. Microsoft and Palo Alto Networks confirmed campaigns targeting government and public-sector users, delivering malware like Havoc C2 and RMM tools.

Paragraph 2: This attack technique undermines traditional URL inspection and user education (e.g., “check where the link points”), as the initial domain is legitimate. It represents a new class of identity-based phishing that is difficult to detect with signature-based tools. Organizations must implement behavioral analysis, OAuth app whitelisting, and user training on unexpected redirects—even from trusted domains. Microsoft has disabled several malicious OAuth apps but warns the campaign is ongoing.

breaking_news.title: University of Hawaiʻi Cancer Center Ransomware Attack Exposes 1.2M Individuals
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: A ransomware attack on the University of Hawaiʻi Cancer Center (UHCC) compromised data of 1.24 million individuals, including Social Security numbers, driver’s license information, voter registration records dating to 1993, and health-related research data. The breach occurred on August 31, 2025, affecting systems supporting the Multiethnic Cohort (MEC) Study but not clinical operations. UHCC engaged threat actors to obtain a decryption tool and assurances data was destroyed—though it remains unclear if a ransom was paid.

Paragraph 2: This incident highlights the growing targeting of academic and medical research entities, which often store highly sensitive longitudinal data with weak security postures. The scale and sensitivity of the exfiltrated data—especially historical voter and health records—pose long-term identity theft and privacy risks. Affected individuals face years of monitoring and potential fraud. The breach also underscores the need for robust backup integrity and air-gapped isolation in research IT environments.
threat_actors: Cl0p ransomware group (attributed via TTPs and leak site activity)

breaking_news.title: Google Chrome Vulnerability CVE-2026-0628 Enabled Extensions to Hijack Gemini Live AI Panel
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: A high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-0628, CVSS 8.8) in Google Chrome allowed malicious extensions to hijack the Gemini Live in Chrome AI assistant panel. The flaw enabled extensions to inject code into the privileged Gemini side panel, inheriting its access to local files, camera, microphone, and screenshots. Google patched the issue in early January 2026 after disclosure by Palo Alto Networks’ Unit 42.

Paragraph 2: This vulnerability exemplifies the expanding attack surface introduced by agentic AI in browsers. As AI assistants gain deeper system access, flaws in their isolation boundaries become high-impact. Attackers could have used this to spy on users, steal credentials, or manipulate AI-generated content. Users of Chrome with Gemini Live enabled—especially enterprise users—should ensure they’re on a patched version and audit installed extensions for legitimacy.
affected_entities: Chrome Users (especially enterprise and AI power users)

breaking_news.title: Iran-Nexus Threat Actors Target Surveillance Cameras Across Middle East Post-Missile Strikes
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Multiple Iran-nexus threat actors have launched a campaign targeting internet-connected surveillance cameras in Israel and neighboring Middle Eastern countries since February 28, 2026. Check Point researchers observed credential stuffing and default credential attacks against IP camera systems, likely to establish surveillance infrastructure and disrupt security operations in conflict zones.

Paragraph 2: This activity reflects the weaponization of IoT devices in geopolitical conflicts, enabling real-time reconnaissance and potential physical sabotage. With over 149 hacktivist DDoS attacks reported in the same timeframe, the cyber domain has become a critical front. Organizations operating in the region must prioritize network segmentation, credential hygiene, and continuous monitoring of external-facing IoT devices.

unique_threat_actors: Unknown (likely APTs or high-skill threat actors)
Cl0p ransomware group (attributed via TTPs and leak site activity)
Unknown forum operators; broad spectrum of cybercriminals
unique_affected_entities: Chrome Users (especially enterprise and AI power users)
